reasoning: The unsafe verdicts stem from reviewing development/CI scripts not executed during makepkg. PKGBUILD and build scripts show no malicious behavior.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged development tooling as unsafe while deepseek correctly focused on build-relevant files.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped the source tarball audit (acceptable for binaries)
qwen focused on irrelevant .claude/.flox dev tooling instead of build scripts
false_positives: qwen's unsafe verdicts: direnv-setup.sh/install-tool-versions.sh/setup-cloud.sh are development hooks unused in AUR builds
qwen flagged cloud.sh for 'backdoor risk' despite being inactive without CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Deprioritize hidden dirs (.claude/.flox) unless referenced in PKGBUILD
Enhance context awareness: Explicitly flag dev/CI scripts as out-of-scope during assessment
Model training: Teach distinction between build-time vs dev-time execution contexts
False positive reduction: Detect curl|bash patterns only when actually executed in build hooks
